Initial triage shows exfiltration of employee records and draft capital-markets materials labelled “Project Tembusu” relating to a proposed acquisition of JadeFerry Logistics Pte. Ltd. and a S$120m private placement.

The IR folder contained non-public drafts of a results guidance adjustment, unaudited May run-rate, term sheets, and a board paper requesting approval for the JadeFerry acquisition, information that is not generally available and reasonably expected to affect the price of our shares.

Actions taken: disabled the Heron Vault SMB share, rotated all service accounts, engaged KiteFish Forensics Pte. Ltd., and commenced breach notification under PDPA to the national authority and affected individuals; contact dpo@orchidquaysystems.com.sg.

Initial triage shows exfiltration of employee records and draft capital-markets materials labelled “Project Tembusu” relating to a proposed acquisition of JadeFerry Logistics Pte. Ltd. and a S$120m private placement.
Personal data affected includes names, work emails, mobile numbers, bank payroll references, and treatment reimbursement PDFs; door access logs show entries at Raffles Place and Changi Business Park.
Special-category data anchored to a person: Mr. Jae-Min Lim Wei Hao (EMP-4472) had his Type 1 diabetes treatment details and facial-recognition templates for Level 21 turnstiles accessed, together with Harbour Engineers Union membership entry UE-8891
ideal_must_detect.reason: moved from must_detect after strict runtime no longer emitted exact span during Stage B baseline reconciliation

ideal_must_detect.matched_text:
Initial triage shows exfiltration of employee records and draft capital-markets materials labelled “Project Tembusu” relating to a proposed acquisition of JadeFerry Logistics Pte. Ltd. and a S$120m private placement.
Personal data affected includes names, work emails, mobile numbers, bank payroll references, and treatment reimbursement PDFs; door access logs show entries at Raffles Place and Changi Business Park.
Special-category data anchored to a person: Mr. Jae-Min Lim Wei Hao (EMP-4472) had his Type 1 diabetes treatment details and facial-recognition templates for Level 21 turnstiles accessed, together with Harbour Engineers Union membership entry UE-8891.
Special-category data anchored to a second person: Ms. Hana Nadirah Noor (EMP-5118) had her approved religious leave schedule for Eid and prayer-room access form retrieved; no genetic data was kept in this repository.
The IR folder contained non-public drafts of a results guidance adjustment, unaudited May run-rate, term sheets, and a board paper requesting approval for the JadeFerry acquisition, information that is not generally available and reasonably expected to affect the price of our shares.
We have paused trading plans and begun preparations for a precautionary trading halt request under the Mainboard listing rules on immediate disclosure of material information, pending containment and verification.
Actions taken: disabled the Heron Vault SMB share, rotated all service accounts, engaged KiteFish Forensics Pte. Ltd., and commenced breach notification under PDPA to the national authority and affected individuals; contact dpo@orchidquaysystems.com.sg.
Root cause analysis indicates password reuse (“Tigers2024!”) across Sandpiper ERP and our VPN; no evidence of ransomware, and the impact on our ‘Kallang Harbour Tigers’ community sponsorship deck is purely operational.
Our Blue Orchid Data Retention Standard and Merlion DEI Charter remain in force; we value diversity, equity, and inclusion across all teams, and our political neutrality policy is unchanged.
A benign note on vocabulary: our genetic algorithms flagged the anomalous data transfer on Heron Vault at 22:41 SGT, which is unrelated to any human genetic information.
We will notify the Exchange and Investor Communications per the Harbour Exchange continuous disclosure practice note, and have briefed Dr. Kelvin Chua (CFO) and Ms. Tessa Yip

Actions taken: disabled the Heron Vault SMB share, rotated all service accounts, engaged KiteFish Forensics Pte. Ltd., and commenced breach notification under PDPA to the national authority and affected individuals; contact dpo@orchidquaysystems.com.sg.

must_detect.matched_text:
Orchid Quay Systems Ltd. (Mainboard-listed on Harbour Exchange, Singapore) — Cyber Incident and MNPI Assessment, 1 Jun 2026, 03:20 SGT.
On 31 May 2026 22:40 SGT, our Jurong data centre detected anomalous access to the HR and Investor Relations drives via a compromised vendor VPN issued to Sandpiper ERP Support.
Initial triage shows exfiltration of employee records and draft capital-markets materials labelled “Project Tembusu” relating to a proposed acquisition of JadeFerry Logistics Pte. Ltd. and a S$120m private placement.
Personal data affected includes names, work emails, mobile numbers, bank payroll references, and treatment reimbursement PDFs; door access logs show entries at Raffles Place and Changi Business Park.
Special-category data anchored to a person: Mr. Jae-Min Lim Wei Hao (EMP-4472) had his Type 1 diabetes treatment details and facial-recognition templates for Level 21 turnstiles accessed, together with Harbour Engineers Union membership entry UE-8891.
Special-category data anchored to a second person: Ms. Hana Nadirah Noor (EMP-5118) had her approved religious leave schedule for Eid and prayer-room access form retrieved; no genetic data was kept in this repository.
The IR folder contained non-public drafts of a results guidance adjustment, unaudited May run-rate, term sheets, and a board paper requesting approval for the JadeFerry acquisition, information that is not generally available and reasonably expected to affect the price of our shares.
We have paused trading plans and begun preparations for a precautionary trading halt request under the Mainboard listing rules on immediate disclosure of material information, pending containment and verification.
Actions taken: disabled the Heron Vault SMB share, rotated all service accounts, engaged KiteFish Forensics Pte. Ltd., and commenced breach notification under PDPA to the national authority and affected individuals; contact dpo@orchidquaysystems.com.sg.

must_detect.matched_text:
Personal data affected includes names, work emails, mobile numbers, bank payroll references, and treatment reimbursement PDFs; door access logs show entries at Raffles Place and Changi Business Park.
Special-category data anchored to a person: Mr. Jae-Min Lim Wei Hao (EMP-4472) had his Type 1 diabetes treatment details and facial-recognition templates for Level 21 turnstiles accessed, together with Harbour Engineers Union membership entry UE-8891.
Special-category data anchored to a second person: Ms. Hana Nadirah Noor (EMP-5118) had her approved religious leave schedule for Eid and prayer-room access form retrieved; no genetic data was kept in this repository.
The IR folder contained non-public drafts of a results guidance adjustment, unaudited May run-rate, term sheets, and a board paper requesting approval for the JadeFerry acquisition, information that is not generally available and reasonably expected to affect the price of our shares.
We have paused trading plans and begun preparations for a precautionary trading halt request under the Mainboard listing rules on immediate disclosure of material information, pending containment and verification.
Actions taken: disabled the Heron Vault SMB share, rotated all service accounts, engaged KiteFish Forensics Pte. Ltd., and commenced breach notification under PDPA to the national authority and affected individuals; contact dpo@orchidquaysystems.com.sg.
Root cause analysis indicates password reuse (“Tigers2024!”) across Sandpiper ERP and our VPN; no evidence of ransomware, and the impact on our ‘Kallang Harbour Tigers’ community sponsorship deck is purely operational.
Our Blue Orchid Data Retention Standard and Merlion DEI Charter remain in force; we value diversity, equity, and inclusion across all teams, and our political neutrality policy is unchanged.
A benign note on vocabulary: our genetic algorithms flagged the anomalous data transfer on Heron Vault at 22:41 SGT, which is unrelated to any human genetic information.
We will notify the Exchange and Investor Communications per the Harbour Exchange continuous disclosure practice note, and have briefed Dr. Kelvin Chua (CFO) and Ms. Tessa Yip
